# Patrick Andrei V. Tolentino
**Senior Java Engineer | Agentic SDLC Architect**
📧 patrick.v.tolentino@gmail.com 📱 +63 915 342 7441 🔗 LinkedIn Profile 📅 Date of Birth: May 30, 1989

## Education
**University of the East**
Bachelor of Science in Information Technology (2007–2011)

## Professional Summary
Experienced Senior Java Engineer with over 12 years in the full software development lifecycle, specializing in the design and delivery of high security microservices within the superannuation and financial sectors. Currently a Senior Consultant at Deloitte, I lead technical initiatives for one of the largest superannuation funds in AU, overseeing a suite of microservices migrated to Java 21 and Spring Boot 3.x.

My core differentiator is the architecting of custom Agentic SDLC frameworks that utilize structured governance gates to significantly accelerate development throughput while ensuring high test coverage and strict compliance. I bridge the gap between legacy stability and modern AI-driven execution, specializing in Java 21, Spring Security, and high-scale microservice orchestration.

### Senior Consultant – Deloitte Consulting Philippines Delivery Center, Inc. (Oct 2021 – Present)

    * Investments
    * Contributions / Direct Debit

* Maintains automated test cases to ensure 70% coverage

* And to make changes as well like library updates to address CVE vulnerabilities
* Migrated microservices from jdk11 to jdk21 with regular security updates
* I was tasked to work on migrating all microservices from jdk11 to jdk21 by batches.
* This was one of my biggest contributions to the project aside from the member report generation I made
* I also worked heavily with the Investments microservice which I implemented that help a user make investment switches according to his preferences
* Manages on Prod release preparation / support
* I oversee the editing of production run sheets to monitor if each developer that have tickets going to prod are listed
* I also manage the pull requests that will serve as artifacts for the pre-production and production deployment (helm version, configurations for each microservice)
* Architected a custom agent for speeding up development with strong governance gates
* Create a custom agent from scratch by making it analyze all the existing code repositories of our client and making standards, guardrails, context gates and architecture decisions out of it
    * Refined it by continuously improving the agentic workflow
    * This agent is a testament to my long exposure and deep understanding of the multiple microservices I have worked with as this copilot agent has helped developers speed up to 40% after adopting this agentic AI tool.
    * This caused to shift the performance regime to a higher level in terms of delivery cycle.

**Defect Management**
* I utilize a plethora of tools to understand where the issue might come from as error codes from microservices can be really difficult to pinpoint especially if the error is found within internal deep API calls.
* I rely on checking logs from Kubernetes
* I also use application insights
* We use x-correlation-ids that help developers speed up sift through error logs for efficiency
* I use postman to replicate all possible scenarios to help in finding the root cause (such as sources of 400 bad request, 500 internal server errors)
